comment_text.sn15.6:1.5: The purpose of these similes is to impress on the mind the incomprehensible vastness of existence. While it is doubtful they were ever meant to be literal numbers, I have done the math in a few cases. By way of comparison, the age of the universe in modern physical cosmology is around 13.9 billion years.
comment_text.sn15.6:2.5: Assuming 2mm per mustard seed, and 10km per league, that’s about 5×10²² years.

root_text.sn15.6:1.2: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn15.6:1.5: So na sukaro saṅkhātuṁ ettakāni vassāni iti vā …pe… ettakāni vassasatasahassāni iti vā”ti.
root_text.sn15.6:2.3: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, āyasaṁ nagaraṁ yojanaṁ āyāmena yojanaṁ vitthārena yojanaṁ ubbedhena, puṇṇaṁ sāsapānaṁ guḷikābaddhaṁ.
root_text.sn15.6:2.4: Tato puriso vassasatassa vassasatassa accayena ekamekaṁ sāsapaṁ uddhareyya.
root_text.sn15.6:2.5: Khippataraṁ kho so, bhikkhu, mahāsāsaparāsi iminā upakkamena parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya, na tveva kappo.
root_text.sn15.6:2.7: Evaṁ dīghānaṁ kho, bhikkhu, kappānaṁ neko kappo saṁsito, nekaṁ kappasataṁ saṁsitaṁ, nekaṁ kappasahassaṁ saṁsitaṁ, nekaṁ kappasatasahassaṁ saṁsitaṁ.

translation_text.sn15.6:1.2: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn15.6:1.5: It’s not easy to calculate how many years, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of years it lasts.”
translation_text.sn15.6:2.1: “But sir, is it possible to give a simile?”
translation_text.sn15.6:2.3: “Suppose there was an iron citadel, a league long, a league wide, and a league high, full of mustard seeds pressed into balls.
translation_text.sn15.6:2.4: And as each century passed someone would remove a single mustard seed.
translation_text.sn15.6:2.5: By this means the huge heap of mustard seeds would be used up before the eon comes to an end.
translation_text.sn15.6:2.6: That’s how long an eon is.
translation_text.sn15.6:2.7: And we’ve transmigrated through many such eons, many hundreds, many thousands, many hundreds of thousands.
translation_text.sn15.6:2.9: This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.6:2.10: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
